title: deny Write(assets/**) ONLY is a NO-OP -> neither Write nor Edit is blocked (a dir-glob Write deny joins P3's no-op family)

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $CASE_DIR/assets/data.txt with the content 'v=1'. Just make the tool call, no commentary.

First use the Read tool to read $CASE_DIR/assets/note.txt. Then use the Edit tool to replace the text 'hello' with 'EDIT_APPLIED' in $CASE_DIR/assets/note.txt. Just make the tool calls, no commentary.

notes: Attribution control for G3 (a denies BOTH Write+Edit; here only Write). MEASURED (results/control.json, neutral /tmp headless -p acceptEdits, 5 trials): deny Write(assets/**) -> 5/5 written = NO-OP. It blocks NEITHER the Write tool (5/5) NOR the Edit tool. The Write dir-glob deny joins P3's family of no-op Write-deny forms (Write(**), exact path). In-repo the harness headless also measured ALLOWED for the Write probe (file created), confirming the no-op. NOTE on SDK: with canUseTool registered, the Write ask surfaces to the callback (fires) -- that is normal acceptEdits ask-surfacing, not the deny rule taking effect; without a callback (plain headless -p) acceptEdits simply auto-approves and the write succeeds. Contrast a3: deny Edit(assets/**) DOES block the Write tool (0/5, edit-family).
hypothesis: A single dir-glob deny Write(assets/**) is a NO-OP: it blocks neither Write (5/5 written) nor Edit. This corrects the confound in a (deny both) -- the block in a is NOT from the Write rule. The Write dir-glob deny behaves like P3's no-op forms.
